designations.designation: ESI Heat Exposure - Surface temperature anomaly
Over the past 30 days, observed deviation from baseline surface temperature

definitions.definition: Difference between local surface temperature and surrounding rural baseline. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Heat Exposure - Heat wave intensity index
During the last 12 months, severity score for prolonged heat events

definitions.definition: Composite measure of heat wave duration and peak temperature. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Heat Exposure - Impervious surface fraction
In the past 7 days, proportion of land covered by sealed surfaces

definitions.definition: Percentage of land area covered by pavement, buildings, and other impervious materials. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Heat Exposure - Building albedo value
Over the past 30 days, solar reflectivity of building exterior materials

definitions.definition: Ratio of reflected to incident solar radiation on building facades. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Heat Exposure - Anthropogenic heat flux
During the last 12 months, rate of heat released by human activities

definitions.definition: Thermal energy per unit area emitted by vehicles, buildings, and industry. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Heat Exposure - Heat-related health impact score
In the past 7 days, composite health burden from elevated urban temperatures

definitions.definition: Aggregate score of heat-related hospital admissions and mortality. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: CRAT Infrastructure Vulnerability - Infrastructure thermal stress rating
Over the past 30 days, risk level of thermal damage to built structures

definitions.definition: Rating of cumulative heat stress on roads, bridges, and utilities. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Infrastructure Vulnerability - Pavement surface temperature
During the last 12 months, temperature of road and sidewalk surfaces

definitions.definition: Measured temperature of asphalt or concrete pavement under solar load. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Infrastructure Vulnerability - Green roof coverage area
In the past 7 days, extent of rooftop vegetation installations

definitions.definition: Total area of building rooftops with installed vegetated cover. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Infrastructure Vulnerability - Urban tree canopy extent
Over the past 30 days, proportion of overhead tree cover in the urban area

definitions.definition: Percentage of urban land area shaded by tree canopy. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Infrastructure Vulnerability - Surface temperature at monitoring point
During the last 12 months, ground-level temperature recorded at the station

definitions.definition: Temperature reading from a calibrated sensor at the measurement site. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Infrastructure Vulnerability - Pedestrian thermal comfort index
In the past 7 days, perceived heat stress for outdoor pedestrians

definitions.definition: Index combining air temperature, humidity, wind, and radiation exposure. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: Airborne particulate concentration
Fine particle mass per unit volume of outdoor air

definitions.definition: Mass concentration of suspended particulate matter in the urban atmosphere. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Heat island spatial extent
Geographic area exhibiting elevated temperatures

definitions.definition: Mapped area where surface temperature exceeds the regional average. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Microclimate zone classification
Local climate type within the urban environment

definitions.definition: Assignment of sub-areas to local climate zone categories. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Monitoring station coordinates
Over the past 30 days, geographic position of the measurement station

definitions.definition: Latitude and longitude of the urban heat monitoring location, assessed over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Seasonal urban heat pattern
During the last 12 months, recurring annual cycle of heat island intensity

definitions.definition: Variation in urban heat island magnitude across seasons, assessed during the last 12 months.

designations.designation: Heat threshold exceedance count
Days exceeding the regulatory heat limit

definitions.definition: Number of days the temperature exceeded the regulatory action level.

designations.designation: Thermal sampling methodology
Procedure for collecting temperature measurements

definitions.definition: Standardized method for obtaining representative thermal readings.

designations.designation: Cool pavement adoption rate
Proportion of streets using reflective paving materials

definitions.definition: Fraction of roadway area resurfaced with high-albedo pavement.

designations.designation: ESI Water Stress - Peak discharge volume
Over the past 30 days, maximum volume of stormwater flowing from the catchment during a storm event

definitions.definition: The highest instantaneous flow volume recorded at the outfall during a single storm event, measured in cubic meters per second. Peak discharge determines channel capacity requirements and downstream flood risk. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Water Stress - Runoff coefficient
During the last 12 months, ratio of runoff to total precipitation over the catchment area

definitions.definition: The dimensionless ratio of surface runoff volume to total rainfall volume for a defined catchment. Higher coefficients indicate greater impervious coverage and reduced infiltration capacity. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Water Stress - First flush pollutant concentration
In the past 7 days, contaminant level in the initial volume of stormwater runoff

definitions.definition: The concentration of suspended solids and dissolved pollutants in the first portion of runoff, which typically carries the highest pollutant load accumulated during the antecedent dry period. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Water Stress - Total suspended solids in runoff
Over the past 30 days, mass of particulate matter carried by stormwater flow

definitions.definition: The total mass of undissolved solid particles per unit volume of stormwater, measured by filtering and weighing. Suspended solids transport adsorbed pollutants and impair receiving water quality. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Water Stress - Pollutant loading rate
During the last 12 months, mass of contaminants delivered per unit time during a storm event

definitions.definition: The rate at which pollutant mass is transported by stormwater flow, expressed in kilograms per hour. Loading rates quantify the contribution of urban runoff to downstream water quality degradation. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Water Stress - Runoff public health risk score
In the past 7 days, health hazard rating from contaminated stormwater exposure

definitions.definition: Composite score reflecting the likelihood of adverse health outcomes from contact with or ingestion of contaminated stormwater runoff. Integrates pathogen counts, heavy metal concentrations, and exposure duration. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: CRAT Environmental Burden - Storm drain network capacity
Over the past 30 days, maximum throughput of the underground stormwater conveyance system

definitions.definition: The design flow capacity of the stormwater drainage network, expressed in cubic meters per second. Capacity shortfalls lead to surface flooding during high-intensity rainfall events. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Environmental Burden - Impervious area ratio
During the last 12 months, fraction of the sub-catchment covered by non-permeable surfaces

definitions.definition: The proportion of the drainage area occupied by roads, rooftops, and other sealed surfaces that prevent rainfall infiltration. Higher ratios increase runoff volume and reduce groundwater recharge. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Environmental Burden - Green infrastructure retention volume
In the past 7 days, stormwater volume retained by bioretention and permeable features

definitions.definition: The volume of stormwater captured and held by green infrastructure elements such as bioswales, rain gardens, and permeable pavements before gradual release or infiltration into the soil. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Environmental Burden - Bioswale infiltration rate
Over the past 30 days, rate at which stormwater percolates through the bioswale soil medium

definitions.definition: The volume of water passing through the engineered soil profile of a bioswale per unit time and area, measured in centimeters per hour. Infiltration rate determines the system's capacity to handle runoff. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Environmental Burden - Runoff water temperature
During the last 12 months, thermal state of stormwater at the point of discharge

definitions.definition: Temperature of stormwater measured at the outfall, expressed in degrees Celsius. Elevated runoff temperatures from heated impervious surfaces can cause thermal pollution in receiving streams. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Environmental Burden - Curb inlet hydraulic efficiency
In the past 7 days, proportion of gutter flow captured by the curb inlet

definitions.definition: The fraction of approaching gutter flow that enters the storm drain through the curb opening. Efficiency depends on inlet geometry, gutter slope, and flow depth relative to the opening dimensions. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: Suspended sediment concentration in runoff
Mass of eroded soil particles carried per unit volume of stormwater

definitions.definition: The concentration of mineral sediment particles suspended in stormwater flow, reported in milligrams per liter. Sediment loading reflects upstream erosion rates and land disturbance. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Rainfall intensity-duration curve
Relationship between rainfall rate and storm duration for design events

definitions.definition: A mathematical function describing how maximum rainfall intensity decreases as storm duration increases for a given return period. Used to size stormwater infrastructure. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Watershed delineation boundary
Topographic boundary defining the contributing drainage area

definitions.definition: The hydrologic boundary enclosing all land area that drains to a common outlet point. Delineated from elevation data using flow accumulation algorithms. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Outfall monitoring coordinates
Over the past 30 days, geographic position of the stormwater discharge monitoring point

definitions.definition: Latitude and longitude of the stormwater outfall where flow and water quality measurements are collected. Coordinates enable spatial analysis of discharge impacts, assessed over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Seasonal rainfall-runoff pattern
During the last 12 months, recurring annual cycle of stormwater generation and quality variation

definitions.definition: The characteristic seasonal pattern of stormwater volume and pollutant loading that reflects the combined influence of precipitation frequency, antecedent moisture, and vegetation cover throughout the year, assessed during the last 12 months.

designations.designation: Discharge permit compliance status
Whether stormwater discharge meets regulatory permit conditions

definitions.definition: An indicator of whether monitored stormwater quality and quantity parameters satisfy the conditions specified in the discharge permit. Non-compliance triggers corrective action requirements.

designations.designation: Stormwater sampling protocol
Standardized procedure for collecting representative runoff samples

definitions.definition: The documented method for obtaining stormwater samples during a storm event, including timing, container preparation, preservation, and chain-of-custody procedures.

designations.designation: Combined sewer overflow frequency
Number of overflow events from the combined sewer system per year

definitions.definition: The annual count of events in which the combined sewer system discharges untreated mixed stormwater and sewage to receiving waters because inflow exceeds treatment plant capacity.

designations.designation: ESI Air Contamination - Indoor carbon dioxide concentration
Over the past 30 days, level of CO2 measured in the occupied indoor space

definitions.definition: The concentration of carbon dioxide in the indoor air of an occupied building, measured in parts per million using a non-dispersive infrared sensor. Carbon dioxide accumulates from human respiration and combustion sources and serves as a primary indicator of ventilation adequacy. Concentrations exceeding one thousand parts per million are associated with occupant complaints of stuffiness, impaired concentration, and reduced cognitive performance. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Air Contamination - Formaldehyde emission level
During the last 12 months, concentration of formaldehyde released from building materials

definitions.definition: The airborne concentration of formaldehyde in an indoor environment, reported in micrograms per cubic meter. Formaldehyde is emitted by pressed-wood products, adhesives, insulation materials, and some textiles. Chronic exposure at elevated concentrations is associated with respiratory irritation, sensitization, and is classified as a known human carcinogen by the International Agency for Research on Cancer. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Air Contamination - Indoor radon activity concentration
In the past 7 days, radioactivity level from radon gas accumulated in the enclosed space

definitions.definition: The activity concentration of radon-222 in indoor air, measured in becquerels per cubic meter using continuous radon monitors or charcoal canisters. Radon enters buildings through cracks in foundations and accumulates in poorly ventilated basements. Prolonged exposure to elevated radon concentrations is the second leading cause of lung cancer after tobacco smoking, and mitigation is recommended when levels exceed the national action level. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Air Contamination - Viable mold spore count
Over the past 30 days, number of culturable airborne mold spores per unit volume of indoor air

definitions.definition: The concentration of viable fungal spores recovered from indoor air samples using volumetric impaction on culture media, expressed as colony-forming units per cubic meter. Elevated mold spore counts indicate moisture intrusion, inadequate ventilation, or microbial amplification on building materials. Mold exposure is associated with allergic rhinitis, asthma exacerbation, and hypersensitivity pneumonitis in susceptible occupants. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Air Contamination - Indoor PM2.5 mass concentration
During the last 12 months, fine particulate matter level in the indoor air of the building

definitions.definition: The mass concentration of airborne particles with aerodynamic diameter less than 2.5 micrometers, measured in micrograms per cubic meter using a gravimetric or optical particle counter. Indoor PM2.5 originates from cooking, tobacco smoke, candle burning, and infiltration of outdoor air. Chronic exposure to elevated indoor PM2.5 is associated with cardiovascular disease, respiratory illness, and premature mortality. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: ESI Air Contamination - Occupant health symptom prevalence
In the past 7 days, proportion of building occupants reporting health symptoms

definitions.definition: The percentage of building occupants who report experiencing one or more health symptoms attributable to the indoor environment, including headache, eye irritation, respiratory discomfort, and fatigue. Symptom prevalence is assessed using standardized occupant surveys and serves as an indicator of overall indoor environmental quality. Elevated symptom rates may indicate inadequate ventilation, chemical off-gassing, or microbial contamination. As part of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: CRAT Health Impact - Mechanical ventilation rate
Over the past 30 days, volume of outdoor air delivered to the indoor space per unit time

definitions.definition: The flow rate of outdoor air supplied to the occupied zone by the mechanical ventilation system, expressed in liters per second per person or air changes per hour. Adequate ventilation dilutes indoor pollutants generated by occupants, materials, and processes. Ventilation rates below the minimum recommended by applicable standards are associated with increased sick building syndrome symptoms and reduced occupant productivity. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Health Impact - Total volatile organic compound mixture
During the last 12 months, sum of all measured volatile organic compounds in the indoor air

definitions.definition: The aggregate concentration of volatile organic compounds detected in the indoor air, reported in micrograms per cubic meter. The total VOC metric represents the combined contribution of individual compounds emitted by paints, solvents, cleaning products, furnishings, and building materials. While no single compound may exceed its individual guideline, the total mixture may still cause sensory irritation and comfort complaints. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Health Impact - Occupant pollutant exposure duration
In the past 7 days, cumulative time the occupant is exposed to indoor contaminants

definitions.definition: The total time, expressed in hours, during which the building occupant is present in an indoor environment with measurable pollutant concentrations. Exposure duration is a key variable in dose-response models and risk assessments. Longer exposure periods at low concentrations may pose comparable health risks to shorter exposures at higher concentrations, depending on the specific pollutant and its toxicological profile. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Health Impact - Air change rate per hour
Over the past 30 days, number of complete indoor air volume replacements per hour

definitions.definition: The rate at which the total volume of indoor air is replaced with outdoor or filtered air, expressed as the number of complete volume exchanges per hour. Air change rate integrates contributions from mechanical ventilation, natural ventilation through openings, and uncontrolled infiltration through the building envelope. Higher air change rates generally reduce indoor pollutant concentrations but increase energy consumption for heating or cooling. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Health Impact - Indoor air temperature
During the last 12 months, dry-bulb temperature of the air inside the occupied space

definitions.definition: The temperature of the indoor air measured at occupant breathing height using a calibrated dry-bulb thermometer or thermocouple, reported in degrees Celsius. Indoor temperature is a primary determinant of thermal comfort and influences the emission rates of volatile organic compounds from building materials. Maintaining temperature within the recommended comfort range reduces occupant complaints and supports cognitive performance. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: CRAT Health Impact - Relative humidity control effectiveness
In the past 7 days, ability of the HVAC system to maintain target indoor humidity levels

definitions.definition: An assessment of the building's heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system's capacity to maintain indoor relative humidity within the target range, typically forty to sixty percent. Inadequate humidity control promotes mold growth at high humidity and causes respiratory tract irritation and static electricity buildup at low humidity. Effectiveness is evaluated by comparing measured humidity profiles against the design specification. Based on the Community Resilience Assessment Tool (CRAT).

designations.designation: Respirable dust concentration indoors
Mass of inhalable dust particles per unit volume of indoor air

definitions.definition: The concentration of respirable particulate matter in the indoor environment, defined as particles that penetrate the unciliated airways and gas-exchange region of the lung. Measured using size-selective sampling with a cyclone or impactor at the respirable fraction cut point of four micrometers aerodynamic diameter. Sources include construction dust, carpet fibers, paper handling, and infiltration of outdoor dust through the building envelope. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: HVAC filter particulate removal efficiency
Fraction of airborne particles captured by the air handling filter

definitions.definition: The percentage of airborne particles removed from the supply airstream as it passes through the HVAC filtration system. Efficiency is characterized by the Minimum Efficiency Reporting Value rating, which ranges from 1 to 20 for progressively finer particle capture. Higher-rated filters reduce occupant exposure to particulate matter but increase system pressure drop and energy consumption. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Pollutant source identification category
Classification of the primary indoor pollution source

definitions.definition: The categorical assignment of the dominant pollutant source in the indoor environment, such as combustion appliances, building materials, cleaning products, occupant activities, or outdoor air infiltration. Source identification guides the selection of control strategies including source removal, increased ventilation, or air purification. The assessment is based on pollutant fingerprinting and temporal correlation analysis. A field of the Environmental Stress Index (ESI).

designations.designation: Indoor monitoring sensor location
Over the past 30 days, position of the air quality sensor within the building

definitions.definition: The geographic coordinates and floor level of the indoor air quality monitoring sensor, recorded to enable spatial mapping of pollutant gradients within the building. Sensor placement follows guidelines specifying breathing-zone height, minimum distance from walls and windows, and avoidance of direct airflow from supply diffusers. Accurate location metadata supports reproducibility and comparison across monitoring campaigns, assessed over the past 30 days.

designations.designation: Seasonal indoor air quality variation
During the last 12 months, recurring annual cycle of indoor pollutant concentrations

definitions.definition: The characteristic seasonal pattern of indoor air pollutant levels driven by changes in ventilation behavior, outdoor air quality, and building operation mode. Indoor pollutant concentrations typically peak during winter months when reduced ventilation and increased use of heating appliances coincide with lower outdoor air exchange. Summer peaks may occur in buildings with elevated cooling loads that recirculate indoor air, assessed during the last 12 months.

designations.designation: Indoor air standard compliance
Whether measured pollutant levels meet applicable indoor air guidelines

definitions.definition: An assessment of whether the measured concentrations of regulated indoor air pollutants fall within the exposure limits established by occupational health authorities or building performance standards. Compliance is evaluated against both short-term exposure limits for acute effects and long-term reference concentrations for chronic health protection. Non-compliance triggers investigation of sources, ventilation adequacy, and remediation measures.

designations.designation: Indoor air sampling protocol
Standardized method for collecting representative indoor air samples

definitions.definition: The documented procedure for obtaining indoor air samples, including the selection of sampling locations, timing relative to occupancy and HVAC operation, sample collection media, flow rates, and duration. Standardized protocols ensure comparability of results across buildings, seasons, and investigations. The protocol specifies quality assurance measures including field blanks, duplicate samples, and chain-of-custody documentation.

designations.designation: Building envelope airtightness
Rate of uncontrolled air leakage through the building shell

definitions.definition: The air permeability of the building envelope, measured by pressurizing the building to a standard reference pressure and recording the airflow required to maintain that pressure. Results are expressed as air changes per hour at fifty pascals or as equivalent leakage area per unit envelope area. Airtightness determines the rate of uncontrolled infiltration, which affects energy consumption, moisture transport, and the ingress of outdoor pollutants.
